introSection: High Explosive is a travelling steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Zierer currently owned and travelled by German showman Vorlop.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Flitzer (as it was originally named) was built for German showman Grauberger in 1971. Following two seasons of operation the ride was sold to Janny de Vries in the Netherlands. It was subsequently purchased by Piet Leander at the end of the 1976 season.

From the 1984 season it operated at Barry Island Pleasure Park in Wales, UK. While at the park, it was repainted and the pattern on the facade was changed. It also operated at Hull Fair.

The Flitzer returned to the Netherlands for a single season in 1989, before being sold to German showman Karl-Heinz Langenscheidt, who operated the ride on the travelling fairground circuit until 1993. It was replaced by a HUSS Rainbow and exported to Poland.

fullText:
High Explosive is a travelling steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Zierer currently owned and travelled by German showman Vorlop.

Flitzer (as it was originally named) was built for German showman Grauberger in 1971. Following two seasons of operation the ride was sold to Janny de Vries in the Netherlands. It was subsequently purchased by Piet Leander at the end of the 1976 season.

From the 1984 season it operated at Barry Island Pleasure Park in Wales, UK. While at the park, it was repainted and the pattern on the facade was changed. It also operated at Hull Fair.

The Flitzer returned to the Netherlands for a single season in 1989, before being sold to German showman Karl-Heinz Langenscheidt, who operated the ride on the travelling fairground circuit until 1993. It was replaced by a HUSS Rainbow and exported to Poland.
